front: Work: Toru Watanabe's love triangle with the depressive Naoko and the vivacious Midori, named after a Beatles song
Work: the protagonist's roommate is a neurotic geography major nicknamed 'Storm Trooper'
Work: the piano teacher Reiko was accused of being a lesbian by a thirteen-year-old student
Work: published in Germany as Naoko's Smile; often called the Japanese Catcher in the Rye
Work: Toru Okada searches for his missing cat Noboru Wataya
Work: Lieutenant Mamiya recollects witnessing a spy being skinned alive by Mongolians during the war
Work: Toru sits in a dried-out well shown to him by teenager May Kasahara; fends off the influence of Noboru Wataya
Work: a short story about cooking spaghetti to Rossini's The Thieving Magpie and a missing cat became this novel's first chapter
Work: fifteen-year-old Kafka runs away to Miss Saeki's library; the amnesiac Nakata lost his memory in the Rice Bowl Hill Incident
Work: Johnnie Walker harvests cats' souls for a flute; Colonel Sanders is an 'abstract concept' who helps Hoshino find the 'entrance stone'
Work: the boy named Crow calls the protagonist the 'world's toughest fifteen-year-old'; may represent his subconscious
Work: the assassin Aomame and math teacher Tengo in an alternate reality with two moons; Little People construct air chrysalises
Work: Fuka-Eri writes about 'Little People' in the story Air Chrysalis; the cult leader known as 'The Leader'
Work: a hitwoman enters an alternate universe after listening to Janacek's Sinfonietta in a taxi
Work: protagonist searches for a sheep with a star-shaped birthmark; his girlfriend has magical ears
work: A Wild Sheep Chase / Rat Trilogy

front: Work: concludes the Rat Trilogy (with Hear the Wind Sing and Pinball, 1973)
work: A Wild Sheep Chase / Rat Trilogy

front: Work: alternating chapters between Calcutecs vs. Semiotecs and 'The End of the World'; unicorn skulls and INKlings
back: Hard-Boiled Wonderland and the End of the World (Haruki Murakami)
work: Hard-Boiled Wonderland and the End of the World

front: Work: 'Barn Burning' — a mysterious yuppie admits to setting fire to barns; adapted into Lee Chang-dong's Burning (2018)
Work: 'The Second Bakery Attack' — two famished newlyweds rob a McDonald's in the middle of the night
Work: an unnamed portrait painter interacts with the title Mozart-like character; a two-foot-tall figure freed from a pit
Author: translators include Alfred Birnbaum and Jay Rubin
Author: memoir What I Talk About When I Talk About Running (titled after a Raymond Carver story); also translated Carver and Chandler into Japanese
Author: wrote baseball poetry about 'the butts of outfielders' in the Yakult Swallows Poetry Collection
